name: ACM Article (sigconf)
description: ACM article in the sigconf style — suitable for ACM conferences and journals (CHI, SIGCOMM, TOIS, etc.).

sections.title: Title, Authors & CCS Concepts
sections.purpose: ACM front matter — title, authors with affiliations, CCS concepts, and keywords.
sections.formatting_notes: Use ACM CCS taxonomy for concepts. Include the 'ACM Reference Format' block.

sections.title: Abstract
sections.purpose: Self-contained summary describing problem, approach, contribution, and result.

sections.title: Author Keywords
sections.purpose: Provide author-supplied keywords beyond CCS concepts.

sections.title: 1 Introduction
sections.purpose: Motivate the problem and state the contribution.
sections.formatting_notes: Numbered section. End with a roadmap paragraph.
sections.citation_example: [1] A. Author. 2024. Title. In Proc. ACM Conf. X. https://doi.org/...

sections.title: 2 Background and Related Work
sections.purpose: Survey the prior literature and position this work.

sections.title: 3 Method / System Design
sections.purpose: Describe the system or method in implementation-grade detail.
sections.formatting_notes: Use subsections 3.1, 3.2 etc. Reference figures explicitly.

sections.title: 4 Evaluation
sections.purpose: Lay out the empirical study — datasets, baselines, metrics, and protocol.
sections.formatting_notes: Each RQ from the introduction must be answered here.

sections.title: 5 Results
sections.purpose: Report findings keyed to the research questions.
sections.formatting_notes: Use one subsection per RQ. Report effect sizes alongside p-values.

sections.title: 6 Discussion
sections.purpose: Interpret results, surface implications, and acknowledge threats to validity.
sections.formatting_notes: Use subsections for implications and limitations.

sections.title: References
sections.purpose: List cited works in ACM Reference Format.
sections.formatting_notes: ACM Reference Format: Author Surname, Initials. Year. Title. Venue. DOI.
sections.citation_example: [1] J. Doe. 2024. A Title. In Proc. ACM CHI '24. https://doi.org/10.1145/xxxxxxx.yyyyyyy
